title: Coverage And Completion Standard
purpose: Defines investigation coverage statuses and completion gates so agents cannot accidentally treat partial UX discovery as complete.

coverageStatuses.meaning: The branch has been investigated to the declared depth and has linked evidence/spec rows.
The branch is required by scope but no sufficient investigation/spec rows exist yet.
The branch is known but blocked by unresolved evidence, access, runtime setup, or product decision.
The branch is omitted with explicit rationale and is not required for the declared package profile.
The branch exists but is outside this investigation package's declared scope.
The branch depends on user/product confirmation before it can be treated as complete.
The branch has a suspected or specified behavior but still needs runtime observation, test evidence, or other proof.

completionGate.summary: A UX investigation is complete only when every in-scope route, surface, subsurface, hidden lane, requirement branch, scenario branch, and acceptance/proof branch has a coverage row.
requiredBeforeComplete: No in-scope compiled route is missing a coverage row.
No route with UI is missing surface decomposition.
No meaningful surface is missing current UX requirement coverage or an explicit omission.
No hidden UX lane is suspected but untracked.
No pending decision is hidden in notes-only fields.
No missing expected basic is silently omitted.
All intentionally omitted rows have rationale.

rules: Coverage indexes expose incompleteness; they do not force agents to resolve all product decisions.
A pending branch can be acceptable for discovery closeout when it is visible and routed.
Implementation or refactor work must not treat pending current UX discovery as confirmed behavior.
Desired UX authoring must not proceed from unknown current behavior without recording the uncertainty.

Discovery closeout must say which discovered behavior was captured in App Behavior, which remains observed-only, which is ideal/future or out of scope, which is runtime drift, which is pending decision, and which bounded gaps remain.

Coverage is not complete for implementation readiness when behavior exists only in source/runtime inventories, audit notes, proof notes, or chat without an App Behavior destination or bounded reconciliation gap.
